name: Christmas Rocks
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 C). Prepare a baking sheet lined with parchment paper or greased.
2. In a large bowl, sift together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, and spices.
3. Cut candied fruits, dates, and figs into small pieces and toss them with a small amount of the flour mixture.
4. Add the pecans, raisins, and currants to the same bowl. Stir until combined.
5. In another large bowl, beat butter and sugar on high speed until light and fluffy, about 2 minutes.
6. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition. Mix in the coffee. Be careful not to overmix.
7. Gently fold the flour mixture into the batter using a spatula or wooden spoon.
8. Using an ice cream scoop or spoon, drop mounds of batter onto the prepared baking sheet, leaving about 2 inches between each cookie.
9. Bake for 14 to 16 minutes, or until set and lightly browned on top.
10. Remove from oven and let cool completely on a wire rack. Store in an airtight container. A small wedge of apple can be placed with them to keep the cookies soft if desired. The cookies may also be glazed or dusted with confectioners' sugar, if desired.
